a: A popover-based interface for selecting a contact.
The methods that you implement to respond to contact-picker user events.
A view controller that displays an interface for picking contacts.
A controller that manages “Saved AutoFill Details” visibility logic for a contact card.
A view controller that displays a new, unknown, or existing contact.
Methods you use to respond to user interactions with a contact view controller.
A SwiftUI button that you use to add to the set of contacts someone shares with your app.
A list of access options to display in the contact access button when matching a contact’s name.
A caption option that displays a default value.
A caption option that displays an email address for the contact.
A caption option that displays a phone number for the contact.
A type that customizes the style of a contact access button.
Provide an interface that allows people to display information about their contacts.
Determines whether to display buttons for actions such as sending a text message or initiating a FaceTime call.
Determines whether the user can edit the contact’s information.
The name to use if the contact has no display name.
A style that provides the default system styling of a contact access button.
Closes the popover.
The contact being displayed.
Called when a property of the contact has been selected by the user.
Called after contacts have been selected by the user.
Called after a contact has been selected by the user.
Called after contact properties have been selected by the user.
In iOS, called when the user taps Cancel.
In macOS, called when the contact picker’s popover has closed.
In macOS, called when the contact picker’s popover is about to close.
The contact store from which the contact was fetched or to which it will be saved.
Called when the view has been presented.
Called when the user selects a property.
The picker delegate to be notified when the user chooses a contact.
The delegate to be notified when the user selects a contact or a property.
The delegate to be notified.
Returns the descriptor for all the keys that must be fetched on the contact before setting it on the view controller.
The keys to be displayed when a contact is expanded.
The [CNContact](/documentation/Contacts/CNContact) property keys to display in the contact detail card.
The contact property keys to display.
Highlights the property of the contact being displayed.
Initializes a view controller for an existing contact.
Initializes a view controller for an existing contact.
Initializes a view controller for a new contact.
Initializes a view controller for an unknown contact.
Creates a contact access button with the provided styling values.
Creates a contact access button to quickly add contacts under limited-access authorization.
The message displayed under the name of the contact.
The container in which to add a new contact.
The group in which to add a new contact.
A predicate to determine the contact selectability in the list of contacts.
A predicate to control the return of the selected contact.
A predicate to control the properties of the selected contact.
Determines whether to display data from contacts that are linked to the contact being displayed.
Shows the picker popover anchored to the specified view.
